Netherlands|Japan.style: Moderate, communicative MLS-school official; manages players verbally, average card and penalty rates. Host-nation referee.

Saudi Arabia|Uruguay.style: Strict, card-happy Serie A official (career avg ~4.8, recent seasons ~3.8-4.1); tight tactical-foul management.

Szymon Marciniak.style: Strict but respected; firm authority, manages big games well. Refereed 2022 WC final and 2023 UCL final; IFFHS best referee 2023 & 2024.

Michael Oliver.style: On the stricter side for the EPL but consistent; lets some play flow, strong game management. ~3.2-3.5 yellows/game across competitions.

Anthony Taylor.style: Experienced, generally lets play continue; card rate fluctuates by season (2.9 in 2024/25, ~3.8 in 2025/26). Pragmatic English style.

Danny Makkelie.style: Balanced, calm communicator; moderate carder, decisive on penalties. One of UEFA's most trusted big-match officials.

Clement Turpin.style: Lenient on cards but quick to point to the spot — holds the record for most UCL penalties awarded. Lets play on, penalty-prone.

Francois Letexier.style: Rising elite ref (Euro 2024 final); fairly card-happy and willing to give penalties. Card rate climbing (~4.8/game in 2025/26).

Felix Zwayer.style: Strict, card-happy — well above average yellows/game; tight disciplinary leash, fewer straight reds.

Slavko Vincic.style: Slightly above-average carder but sparing with penalties; physical, lets contact go. Big-game UEFA regular.

Istvan Kovacs.style: Card-happy and strict — one of the highest yellow rates among WC refs (~5/game). Reaches for the pocket readily.

Joao Pinheiro.style: Above-average carder, fairly willing on penalties; firm Portuguese-school official back at a WC after 12 years.

Maurizio Mariani.style: Strict, card-happy Serie A official (career avg ~4.8, recent seasons ~3.8-4.1); tight tactical-foul management.

Glenn Nyberg.style: Lenient — among the lowest card and penalty rates of the WC refs; lets play flow, Scandinavian hands-off approach.

Alireza Faghani.style: Experienced, authoritative AFC/Australia veteran; moderate carder, composed and decisive in big matches.

Wilton Sampaio.style: Very card-happy and red-prone (~5 yellows, 0.3 reds/game) — typical high-volume South American officiating; lets games get heated then cards.

Facundo Tello.style: Firm, physical, card-happy and red-prone; known for fiery South American matches (notoriously red-heavy in the Maracanazo brawl). Stingier on penalties.

Cesar Arturo Ramos.style: Notably red-prone (~0.4 reds/game) with above-average yellows; busy CONCACAF veteran, lets games run physical.

Ismail Elfath.style: Moderate, communicative MLS-school official; manages players verbally, average card and penalty rates. Host-nation referee.

Abdulrahman Al-Jassim.style: Generally lenient (domestic avg ~3.0; small international sample skews higher to ~4.8); calm, refereed 2022 WC opener. Figures uncertain due to limited tracked data.

Kevin Ortega.style: Very card-happy AND very penalty-happy (~5 yellows, ~0.5 pens/game) — among the most whistle-active WC refs; strict CONMEBOL style.

Sandro Scharer.style: Above-average carder and fairly penalty-willing; firm, consistent Swiss official.

Espen Eskas.style: Lenient, low-card Scandinavian official; few reds, sparing on penalties, lets play flow.

Mustapha Ghorbal.style: Moderate carder (~3.5-3.9/game); leading CAF official. Reliable penalty/red rates not well tracked by aggregators.

Ma Ning.style: Nicknamed 'Card Master' — reputation as one of Asia's strictest, very card-happy (famously 9 yellows + 3 reds in one 2015 CSL match). Per-game career averages not reliably tracked by European aggregators, but qualitatively strict.

note: 主裁判赛前1-2天才公布；16日后多为TBA。比分模型不因裁判调整，仅作风格提示。
